dominant_palette: black, silver, white, charcoal grey, purple-violet
composition_vocabulary: The upper jewellery tiles are centred and object-isolated against neutral or dark grounds. The lower footwear tiles repeat the same form across a grid in slightly varying colourways — a mood-board-within-a-moodboard logic, treating repetition as study rather than variety.
lighting_quality: Mixed: jewellery tiles use controlled studio light to emphasise surface and relief; footwear tiles are rendered in a diffuse, near-flat digital light that removes shadows and foregrounds silhouette.
material_textures: polished silver, brushed metal, oxidised black metal, matte rubber, glossy polymer, skin
aesthetic_register: The sample straddles luxury talisman and near-future streetwear — the jewellery tier reads as heirloom-meets-body-horror, while the footwear tier pulls toward speculative product design and collector-drop culture.
notably_absent: There are no gemstones, coloured stones, precious settings, or sparkle — the conventionally aspirational language of luxury jewellery (brilliance, faceting, sentiment) is entirely missing.
